description: A stiff sharp point growing from a plant's stem, branch, or leaf is 'kewati'. Everyday use can cross the botanical lines among thorns, spines, and prickles; a field guide can restore the finer distinction. 'tiso' (sharp) supplies the quality, not the structure.
articulatory_notes: A brief closure at the soft palate releases 'ke'. The lips round for voiced 'w' and open into stressed 'a'; dental 't' then closes behind the upper teeth and releases into high-front 'i'.

semantic_domains.nature: a stiff sharp point growing from a plant structure
semantic_domains.physical: a pointed plant structure that can catch or pierce
